title: Thread listing uses isSticky instead of isPinned in orderBy
description: GET /api/forums/[id]/threads orders threads by [{ isSticky: 'desc' }, { createdAt: 'desc' }], while the project-scoped /api/projects/[projectId]/forums/[forumId]/threads/index.ts GET orders by [{ isPinned: 'desc' }, { isSticky: 'desc' }, { createdAt: 'desc' }]. The two routes are inconsistent: a thread pinned via /lock+pin.ts that sets isPinned=true will sort to the top on the project-scoped route but NOT on the non-project-scoped route (which only sees isSticky). The cross-meeting and cross-forum callers (useForumThreads hook) may rely on the non-project-scoped endpoint and silently get the wrong order. Also, schema distinguishes isSticky (legacy/sticky-bubble) vs isPinned (pinned-to-top) so the two routes encode different product intents but ship the wrong combination.
suggested_fix: Align the two list endpoints to the same orderBy: [{ isPinned: 'desc' }, { isSticky: 'desc' }, { updatedAt: 'desc' }] (or createdAt) and add an integration test asserting a freshly pinned thread sorts to position 0 from both routes. Also document the distinction in the schema comment.

title: Thread detail GET exposes author.email of every post author unconditionally
description: GET /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId] includes posts[*].author.email even for members who are not the post author. Combined with the thread-level author.email and the post listing route at /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts, every project member can enumerate the email addresses of every poster in the forum. The legacy comment at the top of this file claims RBAC fixes the PII leak vector, but membership is broad and email is not gated to author-or-admin.
suggested_fix: Either (a) strip email from author selects and rely on name+avatarUrl only, or (b) gate the email field on existing.authorId === session.user.id || isAdmin. Add a regression test that asserts a non-author member sees no email on other users' posts.

title: Post PATCH has no author-or-admin check and no edit window
description: PATCH /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId] (and the duplicate project-scoped route at pages/api/projects/[projectId]/forums/[forumId]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId]/index.ts) only runs assertPostProjectMembership. Any project member — including someone who joined after the post was written — can rewrite the content of any post in the forum. There is also no edit-window guard (no `updatedAt` cutoff, no forum.editWindowMinutes), so a member can edit posts that are years old with no audit trail other than updatedAt.
suggested_fix: Add `if (existing.authorId !== session.user.id && !isAdmin) throw 403` mirroring the project-scoped route. Introduce a forum-level editWindowMinutes config (default 30 min) and reject edits past that window with 403 EDIT_WINDOW_EXPIRED, unless the user is admin. Track prior content in a separate PostRevision table for audit if governance requires.

title: Thread PATCH has no author-or-admin check; any member can lock/sticky/pin
description: PATCH /api/projects/[projectId]/forums/[forumId]/threads/[threadId] lets any project member flip isLocked/isSticky on any thread (no ownership or role check). Locking a thread blocks every other member from posting, so any random member can grief the project by locking popular threads. The dedicated lock.ts and pin.ts endpoints gate on isSystemAdmin (so the destructive operation is correctly locked down), but the generic PATCH bypasses those gates.
suggested_fix: Restrict isSticky/isLocked/isPinned changes on PATCH to session.user.id === thread.authorId || isAdmin. Or, drop isSticky/isLocked/isPinned from the PATCH schema entirely and force callers through the dedicated lock.ts/pin.ts endpoints which already enforce admin-only.

title: Minutes PATCH has no author-or-admin check and no lock-after-meeting-starts guard
description: PATCH /api/meetings/[id]/minutes lets any project member rewrite the meeting minutes forever — even after the meeting starts/ends, even by someone who wasn't the author. There is also no check that meeting.startTime < now (no 'freeze minutes after meeting starts' rule), which the spec implies by 'minutes locking after meeting starts' in the review prompt. The only safeguard is project membership.
suggested_fix: Two-pronged fix: (1) gate PATCH on existing.authorId === session.user.id || isAdmin; (2) introduce a 'minutesLocked' boolean on Meeting or compute it from startTime; reject PATCH with 409 MINUTES_LOCKED when (now >= meeting.startTime) and user is not admin. Add a migration if a new column is needed.

title: Attendee POST lets any project member add arbitrary non-member user IDs
description: POST /api/meetings/[id]/attendees replaces the entire attendee list with body.attendees[*].userId without verifying that each userId is actually a member of the project. A logged-in member can add the userId of any other user in the system (including users outside the project, or external users, or service accounts) and trigger meeting-conflict checks, notification emissions, and PII exposure via the response. assertMeetingProjectMembership only checks the actor's membership, not the invited userIds.
suggested_fix: Before prisma.meetingAttendee.createMany, query prisma.member.findMany({ where: { userId: { in: attendeeIds }, projectId } }) and reject with 400 INVALID_ATTENDEE if the set of returned userIds != the request set. Reuse the same check in /api/meetings/index.ts POST (lines 110-123) which has the same gap.

title: Agenda PATCH position update does not persist order or detect collisions
description: PATCH /api/meetings/[id]/agenda/[agendaId] allows client to set body.position to an arbitrary integer, but the handler writes only that one item's position — sibling items keep their old positions. The result is two items at the same position (or gaps) after every reorder, and the GET endpoint sorts by position asc so the order becomes undefined on collisions. There is no reorderAll variant that takes an ordered id array and persists a contiguous 0..N-1 ordering.
suggested_fix: Replace position=number with position=number+direction, OR add a new POST /api/meetings/[id]/agenda/reorder endpoint that takes { items: [{ id, position }, ...] } and applies a single prisma.$transaction that re-numbers 0..N-1 in array order. In the single-item PATCH, shift neighbors to close gaps when moving down. Document the reorder contract in lib/meeting/agenda.ts.

title: Agenda POST does not enforce agenda uniqueness or accept minutes linkage
description: POST /api/meetings/[id]/agenda creates an item with title+notes+duration+position but never links to a MeetingMinutes entry, even though MeetingAgendaItem would naturally map 1:1 to a minute section. Concurrent POSTs can also create duplicate titles with no constraint, and concurrent posts with the same position value will collide silently (see FM-7).
suggested_fix: Add a unique compound index on (meetingId, position) in schema.prisma, or apply shiftNeighbors logic in a transaction. Add an optional `minutesId` field to MeetingAgendaItem and accept it on POST/PATCH; auto-create a MeetingMinutes stub if missing.

title: Race condition in post-in-locked-thread check (TOCTOU)
description: POST /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts reads thread.isLocked, then issues a separate prisma.forumPost.create. Between the read and the create, a moderator can lock the thread and the new post will still land. The same pattern exists in the project-scoped posts/index.ts (lines 87-107). No SELECT FOR UPDATE or transactional check is used.
suggested_fix: Wrap the create in prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const t = await tx.forumThread.findUnique({ where: { id: threadId }, select: { isLocked: true }}); if (t?.isLocked) throw 403 THREAD_LOCKED; return tx.forumPost.create(...); }). Add a concurrency test that locks the thread between the two reads and asserts 403.

title: Lock toggle flips based on pre-update state with no concurrency guard
description: POST /lock.ts reads existing.isLocked then writes !existing.isLocked. Two concurrent admin clicks (e.g. two browser tabs) both read isLocked=false and both write isLocked=true — the second click is a no-op instead of a true toggle, and the UI shows 'unlocked' even though both clicks happened. No version/optimistic-concurrency field on ForumThread.
suggested_fix: Add a `version Int @default(0)` column on ForumThread (migration required). Update with where: { id, version: existing.version }, data: { isLocked: !existing.isLocked, version: { increment: 1 } }. If updateMany returns count=0, return 409 STALE_VERSION and let the client refetch.

title: Pin toggle has same TOCTOU race as lock.ts
description: Same pattern as FM-10: pin reads existing.isPinned then writes the negation without a version check or transaction. Concurrent pins can lose the second click.
suggested_fix: Same as FM-10: add version column + optimistic concurrency. Or share a single `toggleThreadFlag(threadId, flagKey)` helper used by both lock.ts and pin.ts.

title: Vote toggle has race that can corrupt voteScore counter
description: POST /vote.ts reads existingVote, then either deletes or creates a ForumVote, then computes voteScore = post.voteScore ± 1 and writes it back. Two concurrent vote clicks from the same user race: both reads see no vote, both create a vote (the second fails the unique constraint with a 500 — no try/catch), or one read sees the vote and deletes while the other creates — leaving 2 votes for the same user and an off-by-one score. The score is also recomputed only on this endpoint; if any other path mutates ForumVote (admin script, migration), voteScore goes stale.
suggested_fix: Wrap toggle+score-update in prisma.$transaction with an upsert on ForumVote (deleteMany + createMany where userId+postId), and recompute voteScore as a prisma.forumVote.count({ where: { postId } }) inside the same transaction. Avoid the read-modify-write of voteScore; treat it as a derived column. Add a regression test for concurrent toggles.

title: Attendee POST does not check meeting.startTime — guests can be added after meeting has started
description: POST /api/meetings/[id]/attendees has no guard against modifying attendees after the meeting has started. A user added at T+30min into a T+0 meeting will receive a 'meeting reminder' for a meeting already in progress, and the attendee list becomes a moving target for minutes/audit. The review prompt explicitly calls out 'attendee invitation validation' as in-scope; validation here is incomplete.
suggested_fix: If meeting.startTime < now, reject PATCH/POST attendee changes with 409 MEETING_IN_PROGRESS unless isAdmin. Alternatively, allow only removing attendees (not adding) after startTime. Add a test asserting 409 once startTime is in the past.

title: Meeting POST does not validate startTime in the future (past meetings allowed)
description: createMeetingSchema accepts any valid datetime for startTime, including a time in the past. The project-scoped route at /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/index.ts has the same gap. A user can backdate a meeting (e.g. 'minutes from last week') and the conflict-check logic will run against that past time, potentially conflicting with current meetings via overlap arithmetic.
suggested_fix: Add `startTime` schema refinement: .refine(d => new Date(d) > new Date(Date.now() - 60_000), 'startTime must be in the future or within the last minute'). Allow isAdmin to bypass for backfilling. Add a regression test.

title: Agenda PATCH/DELETE allowed after meeting end (no lifecycle guard)
description: PATCH and DELETE on agenda items run with no check against meeting.startTime or meeting.endTime. A meeting that finished a year ago can still have its agenda silently rewritten, breaking the historical record. POST on agenda is similarly unguarded (a new item can be added to a 6-month-old meeting).
suggested_fix: Reject mutations on agenda items when meeting.endTime < now and !isAdmin with 409 MEETING_CLOSED. For POST (agenda/index.ts) apply the same rule. For consistency, the same guard probably belongs on /api/meetings/[id]/index.ts PATCH (title/startTime/endTime of past meetings).

title: Thread detail GET sorts posts by createdAt asc — no support for admin-pinned posts
description: The thread GET endpoint always sorts by createdAt asc, but the schema's ForumPost model has no `isPinned` or `position` column. In OpenProject classic, a moderator can pin a specific reply to the top of a thread (e.g. to highlight an accepted answer). The current code forces chronological order with no override mechanism.
suggested_fix: Add `position Int?` and `isAnswer Boolean @default(false)` to ForumPost. Order by [{ isAnswer: 'desc' }, { position: 'asc' }, { createdAt: 'asc' }]. Add PATCH support for setting these flags gated to author-or-admin.

title: Meeting PATCH does not check if meeting has started when changing startTime/endTime
description: PATCH /api/meetings/[id] allows moving startTime to a past time, shortening endTime below startTime via separate fields, or any combination that produces an invalid range. The check `endTime <= startTime` only validates the final pair, not the user's intent (e.g. endTime < existing.startTime is rejected but is silently allowed via the same field re-set, depending on order).
suggested_fix: Compare against original startTime: if existing.startTime < now and user is !isAdmin and body.startTime !== existing.startTime, return 409 MEETING_STARTED. Validate duration >= 1 minute. Add lifecycle constants (UPCOMING/IN_PROGRESS/CLOSED) and a single canMutateMeeting() helper.

title: Meeting GET exposes author.email + all attendees.emails unconditionally
description: GET /api/meetings/[id] includes author.email and every attendee.user.email. assertMeetingProjectMembership only checks the actor's project membership, so any project member sees the email list of every other attendee — a classic HR-spam vector and privacy violation. The same pattern is in /api/meetings/index.ts, /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/index.ts, /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/upcoming/index.ts.
suggested_fix: Either (a) strip email from all author/attendee selects and rely on name+avatarUrl, or (b) gate email on isAdmin or session.user.id === author.id/attendee.id. Add an opt-in flag ?includeEmail=me to gate self-only exposure. Add a regression test.

title: RSVP endpoint is implemented but never called by frontend
description: The route header comment (line 18-21) states this endpoint is 'currently unused by the frontend'. RSVP is a critical meeting feature (accept/decline/tentative + comment). It should be wired into frontend/components/meetings (currently an empty dir), and /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/index.ts POST's hardcoded response: 'none' on attendee create bypasses RSVP semantics.
suggested_fix: Wire useMeetingRsvp hook to POST on user action, plumb UI in frontend/components/meetings/MeetingCard.tsx, and update meeting POST to create attendees with response: 'pending' (add a 'pending' enum value to MeetingAttendee.response).

title: Meeting POST accepts attendees without RSVP semantics; conflicts only checked on creation
description: createMeetingSchema accepts `attendees: z.array(z.string())` as bare user IDs (no response/comment). POST /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/[id]/rsvp/index.ts (the RSVP path) is unreachable from the create flow. There is no scheduler / cron to send meeting reminders (no /api/meetings/[id]/reminders or equivalent), and no integration with the email layer (lib/email/).
suggested_fix: Add meeting-reminders table + cron job: 24h-before, 1h-before, on-start. Wire notification emission at meeting creation (already a pattern in lib/activity.ts). Add an OpenProject-style 'send invitation' email via lib/email/. Add endpoints: POST /api/meetings/[id]/reminders (admin-only), GET /api/meetings/[id]/reminders (members).

title: No agenda template support — every meeting rebuilds agenda from scratch
description: OpenProject classic supports agenda templates ('standard', 'status', 'risk review') that pre-populate items on meeting create. No template model exists (verified — not in prisma/schema.prisma). Meetings with similar cadences (e.g. weekly standup) duplicate the same agenda each week.
suggested_fix: Add MeetingAgendaTemplate model: { id, projectId, name, items: Json (array of { title, duration, notesTemplate }) }. POST /api/meetings accepts `agendaTemplateId` and pre-populates meeting_agenda_items via $transaction. Add per-project CRUD for templates.

title: No forum search/subscription functionality
description: Forum routes have no search endpoint, no subscription model, no notification on new post/thread. There is no /api/forums/[id]/search and no ForumSubscription table in schema.prisma. Users have no way to follow a thread and get email/in-app notifications when it updates.
suggested_fix: Add ForumSubscription model { id, userId, threadId (or forumId), createdAt }. POST /api/forums/[id]/subscribe, DELETE to unsubscribe. Add a GET /api/forums/search?q=&projectId= route using Prisma full-text search. On ForumPost create, emit a notification (already a pattern via lib/activity.ts) to all subscribers.

title: No post reactions (emote/like) — schema has ForumVote but no reaction table
description: OpenProject classic and most forum products support reactions (👍 ❤️ 🎉) on posts. The ForumVote model is a single binary upvote (no reaction type), so 'Like' is the only signal. No schema column for reactionType, no UI surface.
suggested_fix: Add ForumReaction { id, postId, userId, kind: String } with @@unique([postId, userId, kind]). PATCH /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId]/reactions with body { kind, toggle }. Replace ForumVote with ForumReaction or keep both. Add tests.

title: No minutes export endpoint (PDF/Markdown)
description: Minutes are stored as plain text content in MeetingMinutes.content (DB column is @db.Text). There is no GET /api/meetings/[id]/minutes/export or equivalent that returns a rendered PDF or Markdown. Users cannot email minutes to stakeholders or attach them to project reports.
suggested_fix: Add GET /api/meetings/[id]/minutes/export?format=pdf|md returning application/pdf or text/markdown. Use a PDF renderer (e.g. puppeteer, react-pdf, or @react-pdf/renderer) wrapped server-side. Cache the rendered output via the existing lib/cache/. Audit export events via lib/activity.ts.

title: Forum GET does not return lastActivityAt / lastPoster — empty forums look identical to active forums
description: Forum list response lacks a 'lastActivity' field (last post timestamp + last poster). Empty forums sort/display indistinguishably from active ones, hurting forum discovery. The data is available (just join posts.orderBy desc limit 1) but not exposed.
suggested_fix: Add `lastPostAt`, `lastPostAuthorId`, `lastPostAuthorName` fields to the response via Prisma aggregation (e.g. posts.orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }, take: 1). Sort the forum list by lastPostAt desc by default. Update frontend to render 'last activity 3h ago by Alice'.

title: Attendee POST replaces all attendees — no incremental add/remove
description: The endpoint deleteMany + createMany semantics silently drop attendees that were present before the request. A frontend that wants to add one user must first GET the full list, append, and POST it back — losing any response/comment state for existing attendees. There is no POST /api/meetings/[id]/attendees/[userId] for incremental operations.
suggested_fix: Add POST /api/meetings/[id]/attendees/{userId} (add or update) and DELETE /api/meetings/[id]/attendees/{userId} (remove). Replace bulk POST with a thin wrapper that loops or upserts. Preserve response/comment state on no-op updates.

title: GET agenda returns raw items with no minutes cross-reference
description: Each MeetingAgendaItem has no minutesId link, so the UI cannot show 'agenda item X was discussed in minutes section Y'. The spec in the review prompt calls this out as 'agenda minute linking' — it's not implemented at the data layer.
suggested_fix: Add `minutesId String?` to MeetingAgendaItem (nullable, FK to MeetingMinutes.id). On POST/PATCH, allow passing minutesId. Add a GET /api/meetings/[id]/agenda?include=minutes that joins MeetingMinutes via the new column.

title: Meeting PATCH accepts new attendee list — but no separate add/remove endpoint
description: Same as FM-26 but at the meeting level. PATCH /api/meetings/[id] does not even accept attendees (schema has no attendees field), so the only way to modify attendees is the bulk POST /api/meetings/[id]/attendees endpoint. There is no /api/meetings/[id]/attendees/[userId] for incremental ops.
suggested_fix: Add attendeeId-scoped endpoints: POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/meetings/[id]/attendees/[userId]. Use upsert semantics so concurrent RSVP and bulk add don't clobber each other. See also FM-26.

title: Test suite has no coverage for sticky/pin ordering, post edit window, thread lock race
description: Reviewed the 603-line forums.unit.test.ts. There is no test asserting sticky-thread ordering (isSticky desc, isPinned desc) on GET /api/forums/[id]/threads. There is no test for the post-edit-window guard (none exists yet — see FM-3). There is no concurrency test for the lock-then-post race (FM-9). Coverage gap is structural: the file uses unit-test mocking patterns that don't exercise Prisma's transactional guarantees.
suggested_fix: Add integration tests in __tests__/api/forums-integration.test.ts that (a) seed a forum with sticky+pinned+normal threads and assert orderBy, (b) call PATCH /posts/[id] as a non-author and expect 403, (c) run two concurrent PATCH /lock calls and assert one returns 409. Use a real Postgres or testcontainers if available.

title: Test suite lacks coverage for meeting start-time validation, agenda reorder persistence, RSVP
description: Reviewed the 512-line meetings.unit.test.ts. No test for `startTime in the past` rejection on meeting POST. No test for concurrent agenda-item PATCH that creates position collisions. No test for the rsvp endpoint at /api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/[id]/rsvp (which the route comment admits is unused). No test for minutes-lock-after-meeting-starts guard.
suggested_fix: Add: (a) meeting-create-past-startTime returns 400; (b) two parallel agenda PATCH calls producing two items at position=5 result in 409; (c) rsvp endpoint returns 200 and sets response='accepted' on attendee; (d) minutes PATCH after meeting.startTime returns 409. Promote rsvp from 'unused' to 'used' or remove the route.

title: Forum DELETE cascades to threads/posts without confirmation or soft-delete
description: DELETE /api/forums/[id] cascades via onDelete: Cascade on ForumThread.forum (schema line 579). All threads and posts in the forum are permanently destroyed in one call. No soft-delete (no deletedAt column), no confirmation token, no audit retention. This is a significant data-loss vector — a single misclick destroys years of discussion history.
suggested_fix: Add deletedAt DateTime? to Forum/ForumThread/ForumPost. Refactor DELETE to soft-delete (set deletedAt = now) and exclude deleted records from GET via Prisma where clause. Add a cron job (or admin endpoint) for hard-delete after N days. Preserve audit log entries.

title: RSVP 'tentative' status mapped but no enum update on schema
description: The RSVP handler accepts status: 'accepted' | 'declined' | 'tentative' but MeetingAttendee.response column is just a String default 'none' (no enum constraint). 'tentative' is silently accepted by zod but written as 'tentative' which is not in the original spec's response values. The GET /api/meetings/[id] returns response but the schema doesn't enforce which strings are valid — future code can write arbitrary garbage.
suggested_fix: Add a Prisma enum MeetingResponse { NONE ACCEPTED DECLINED TENTATIVE } and migrate response column to that type. Update Zod schema and emitActivity usage to reference the enum.

title: Minutes POST does not check meeting exists or is in a creatable state
description: POST /api/meetings/[id]/minutes only asserts project membership and rejects if minutes already exist. It does not verify that the meeting has started (could create minutes for a meeting 6 months in the future) or that the meeting is not deleted. The only prerequisite is 'minutes don't already exist'.
suggested_fix: Add `if (now < meeting.startTime && !isAdmin) throw 409 MEETING_NOT_STARTED`. Verify meeting exists via the assertMeetingProjectMembership helper (already returns projectId; add meeting existence check or do an explicit findUnique).

title: Post creation does not emit a notification to thread subscribers
description: POST /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts creates the post but does not emit any notification to the thread author or to other users subscribed to the thread (no ForumSubscription model exists — see FM-22). The project-scoped posts/index.ts does emit activity but not a per-user notification. Subscribers will miss replies unless they manually reload.
suggested_fix: After createMany/upsert ForumSubscription, emit notifications (via lib/notifications/) to all subscribers. Mirror the activity pattern in lib/activity.ts. Add a Notification row with reason 'forum_reply'.

title: Cross-route DELETE asymmetry: project-scoped deletes as author-or-admin, non-scoped allows any member
description: DELETE /api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId] (the non-project-scoped route) has NO author-or-admin check — only assertPostProjectMembership. The project-scoped version at /api/projects/[projectId]/forums/[forumId]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId]/index.ts (line 127) DOES check `existing.authorId !== session.user.id && !isAdmin`. Two endpoints with the same path purpose, different authz. A user can bypass the authorship check by hitting the wrong route.
suggested_fix: Add the same `existing.authorId !== session.user.id && !isAdmin → 403` check to the non-project-scoped route. Consider deprecating the non-project-scoped route entirely and routing everything through the project-scoped URL for consistency.
